title: Capture before architecture
note: Joel wants 008 built from scratch and wants the lesson trail captured while we build. 006 turned out great, but the missing artifact was the rationale/lesson capture: why guardrails were added, what tradeoffs they encode, and what future learners/agents should remember.
source: user turn: build 008 from scratch / capture lessons as we build

title: Claude planning pass captured
note: Ran claude -p with approved Opus alias for a read-only plan because the current harness model policy does not allow Sonnet-5 yet. The plan argues capture must become a checkpoint gate, not a habit, and lists the human-owned pause points: lifecycle states, recovery policy, agreement definition, incumbent checker authority, and where Effect is load-bearing.

title: Sonnet planning pass captured
note: Updated the global Pi model policy to allow explicit Claude Code Sonnet requests, then ran claude -p --model sonnet in plan mode for the 008 rebuild. Sonnet agreed with the forcing-function shape: capture gate first, seven architecture decisions before TypeScript, typed Effect/XState scaffold with candidate abstaining before behavior, and hard stops for lifecycle/recovery/agreement/incumbent/Effect boundary.

title: Sonnet 006 analysis started
note: Started a background Claude Code Sonnet run to analyze the curated Run 006 receipts and produce a 10-steps-to-complex-learning-inspired build sequence for rebuilding 008. This is explicitly a capture-first planning artifact, not product code.

title: Sonnet 006 analysis rerun with embedded receipts
note: The first Sonnet run stopped with an access question because the Run 006 prompt-slide file lived outside the Claude working directory. Rerunning with the curated Run 006 entries embedded so the output can be receipt-backed without cross-directory reads.

title: Sonnet 006 build sequence completed
note: Background Sonnet rerun completed successfully and wrote a 239-line Run 006 to 008 build-sequence plan. It includes 10 build steps, pause points for Joel, first three commits, and risks; one validation detail needs parent review before treating it as executable.

title: Wzrrd summary page published and Discord DM sent
note: Published a noindex Tufte-style Wzrrd summary page for the Run 006 to 008 build sequence and sent the URL to Joel by Discord DM.

title: Granola summary recovered; raw transcript unavailable
note: Recovered the AIE WF26 Loopcraft Granola document via this Pi session/local Granola app data. Evidence confirms a Granola-generated talk summary and public share page, but the public payload has documentTranscript null and the local document has transcribe disabled/no transcription metadata. Published a corrected Wzrrd page that says Granola-summary-backed, not raw-transcript-backed, and DM’d the corrected URL.
source: Granola local app database copy + public Granola share payload
